devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इति सर्वे सुसंरब्धा वाहानारुह्य दंशिताः । स्वैः स्वैर्बलैः परिक्रान्ता अन्वीयुर्धृतकार्मुकाः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca iti sarve susaṃrabdhā vāhānāruhya daṃśitāḥ svaiḥ svairbalaiḥ parikrāntā anvīyurdhṛtakārmukāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
With these self-condemning words, all the highly indignant kings put on their coats of mail and mounting their own vehicles and surrounded by their respective armies hotly pursued Kṛsṇa, equipped with bows in their hands.

devanagari: तानापतत आलोक्य यादवानीकयूथपाः । तस्थुस्तत्सम्मुखा राजन्विस्फूर्ज्य स्वधनूंषि ते ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tānāpatata ālokya yādavānīkayūthapāḥ tasthustatsammukhā rājanvisphūrjya svadhanūṃṣi te

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: Beholding the on-rushing kings in pursuit, the generals of the Yādava army stood their ground to confront them, twanging their bows, Oh king.

devanagari: अश्वपृष्ठे गजस्कन्धे रथोपस्थेऽस्त्र कोविदाः । मुमुचुः शरवर्षाणि मेघा अद्रिष्वपो यथा ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: aśvapṛṣṭhe gajaskandhe rathopasthe mumucuḥ śaravarṣāṇi meghā adriṣvapo yathā
translation: Veterans of war (like Jarāsandha) who were riding on horse-back, the shoulders of elephants and seats in the chariots discharged volleys of arrows like innumerable clouds pouring showers of water on mountains.

devanagari: पत्युर्बलं शरासारैश्छन्नं वीक्ष्य सुमध्यमा । सव्रीडमैक्षत्तद्वक्त्रं भयविह्वललोचना ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: patyurbalaṃ śarāsāraiśchannaṃ vīkṣya sumadhyamā savrīḍamaikṣattadvaktraṃ bhayavihvalalocanā
translation: Finding her consort’s force covered with showers of arrows, the slenḍer-waisted bride with her eyes agitated with fear looked at her Lord’s face bashfully.

devanagari: प्रहस्य भगवानाह मा स्म भैर्वामलोचने । विनङ्क्ष्यत्यधुनैवैतत्तावकैः शात्रवं बलम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: prahasya bhagavānāha mā sma bhairvāmalocane vinaṅkṣyatyadhunaivaitattāvakaiḥ śātravaṃ balam
translation: Laughing out that fear, the Lord assured her: “Don’t be afraid, Oh fair-eyed maiden! The inimical forces will be liquidated in no time by your soldiers”.

devanagari: तेषां तद्विक्रमं वीरा गदसङ्कर्षणादयः । अमृष्यमाणा नाराचैर्जघ्नुर्हयगजान्रथान् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ tadvikramaṃ vīrā gadasaṅkarṣaṇādayaḥ amṛṣyamāṇā nārācairjaghnurhayagajānrathān
translation: Not tolerating that prowess of the enemies, (the leaders of the Yādava army like) Gaḍa, Balarāma and others shot down the horses, elephants and chariots (of the enemy) with their steel-arrows (nārāca).

devanagari: पेतुः शिरांसि रथिनामश्विनां गजिनां भुवि । सकुण्डलकिरीटानि सोष्णीषाणि च कोटिशः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: petuḥ śirāṃsi rathināmaśvināṃ gajināṃ bhuvi sakuṇḍalakirīṭāni soṣṇīṣāṇi ca koṭiśaḥ
translation: And rolled down on the ground crores of heads of the occupants of chariots, horsemen and elephant-riders—all ornamented with ear-rings, crowns and head-gears.

devanagari: हस्ताः सासिगदेष्वासाः करभा ऊरवोऽङ्घ्रयः । अश्वाश्वतरनागोष्ट्र खरमर्त्यशिरांसि च ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: hastāḥ sāsigadeṣvāsāḥ karabhā ūravo aśvāśvataranāgoṣṭra kharamartyaśirāṃsi ca
translation: And fell down on the grounds, their hands wielding swords, maces (gadās) and bows, and also fore-arms, thighs, feet, as well as the heads of horses, mules, elephants, camels, donkeys and (infantry-) men.

devanagari: हन्यमानबलानीका वृष्णिभिर्जयकाङ्क्षिभिः । राजानो विमुखा जग्मुर्जरासन्धपुरःसराः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: hanyamānabalānīkā vṛṣṇibhirjayakāṅkṣibhiḥ rājāno vimukhā jagmurjarāsandhapuraḥsarāḥ

[vocative single from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: As their armies were thus being drubbed and slain by Vṛṣṇis ambitious of victory, the kings led by Jarāsandha turned back and fled away (from the battle field).

devanagari: शिशुपालं समभ्येत्य हृतदारमिवातुरम् । नष्टत्विषं गतोत्साहं शुष्यद्वदनमब्रुवन् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: śiśupālaṃ samabhyetya hṛtadāramivāturam naṣṭatviṣaṃ gatotsāhaṃ śuṣyadvadanamabruvan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ śuṣ class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śuṣ class 4 verb]

translation: They all approached Śiśupāla who was gloomy, dispirited and with parched-up mouth, grieving as if his legally married wife was abducted.

devanagari: भो भोः पुरुषशार्दूल दौर्मनस्यमिदं त्यज । न प्रियाप्रिययो राजन्निष्ठा देहिषु दृश्यते ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: bho bhoḥ puruṣaśārdūla daurmanasyamidaṃ tyaja na priyāpriyayo rājanniṣṭhā dehiṣu dṛśyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation:
The kings consoled him:
“Oh tiger among men! Give up this despondency. In the case of embodied beings, pleasure or pain is never observed to be his permanent lot.

devanagari: यथा दारुमयी योषित्नृत्यते कुहकेच्छया । एवमीश्वरतन्त्रोऽयमीहते सुखदुःखयोः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā dārumayī yoṣitnṛtyate kuhakecchayā evamīśvaratantro
translation: Just as a wooden doll dances according to the will of the showman (controlling it), the same way, due to his being controlled by God, the embodied being endeavours to pass through pleasure or pain.

devanagari: शौरेः सप्तदशाहं वै संयुगानि पराजितः । त्रयोविंशतिभिः सैन्यैर्जिग्ये एकमहं परम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: śaureḥ saptadaśāhaṃ vai saṃyugāni parājitaḥ trayoviṃśatibhiḥ sainyairjigye ekamahaṃ param

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Though backed by an army of twenty-three akṣauhiṇīs, (every time) I suffered defeat at the hands of Kṛsṇa in seventeen expeditions. But ultimately I was victorious in one (the last) battle.

devanagari: तथाप्यहं न शोचामि न प्रहृष्यामि कर्हिचित् । कालेन दैवयुक्तेन जानन्विद्रावितं जगत् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tathāpyahaṃ na śocāmi na prahṛṣyāmi karhicit kālena daivayuktena jānanvidrāvitaṃ jagat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jñā class 9 verb], [vocative single from √ jñā class 9 verb]

translation: I, however, do not feel aggrieved or elated by anything (defeat or victory) as I know that the world is controlled by time in combination with Providence.

devanagari: अधुनापि वयं सर्वे वीरयूथपयूथपाः । पराजिताः फल्गुतन्त्रैर्यदुभिः कृष्णपालितैः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: adhunāpi vayaṃ sarve vīrayūthapayūthapāḥ parājitāḥ phalgutantrairyadubhiḥ kṛṣṇapālitaiḥ
translation: Leaders of the army-commanders as we are, on this occasion also we were defeated by the small contingent of Yādavas under the protection of Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: रिपवो जिग्युरधुना काल आत्मानुसारिणि । तदा वयं विजेष्यामो यदा कालः प्रदक्षिणः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: ripavo jigyuradhunā kāla ātmānusāriṇi tadā vayaṃ vijeṣyāmo yadā kālaḥ pradakṣiṇaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: The enemies became victorious this time as Time was favourable to them. We too shall vanquish them when the Time will be propitious to us.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं प्रबोधितो मित्रैश्चैद्योऽगात्सानुगः पुरम् । हतशेषाः पुनस्तेऽपि ययुः स्वं स्वं पुरं नृपाः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ prabodhito mitraiścaidyo hataśeṣāḥ punaste

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Thus consoled with enlightenment by his friends, Śiśupāla returned to his capital along with his followers, while the other allied kings who survived that battle went back to their respective cities.

devanagari: रुक्मी तु राक्षसोद्वाहं कृष्णद्विडसहन्स्वसुः । पृष्ठतोऽन्वगमत्कृष्णमक्षौहिण्या वृतो बली ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: rukmī tu rākṣasodvāhaṃ kṛṣṇadviḍasahansvasuḥ pṛṣṭhato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: The mighty prince Rukmī who hated Kṛṣṇa, could not tolerate the idea of his sister being married to Kṛṣṇa according to the rākṣasa from of marriage and with an army of one akṣauhiṇī, he hotly pursued Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: रुक्म्यमर्षी सुसंरब्धः शृण्वतां सर्वभूभुजाम् । प्रतिजज्ञे महाबाहुर्दंशितः सशरासनः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: rukmyamarṣī susaṃrabdhaḥ śṛṇvatāṃ sarvabhūbhujām pratijajñe mahābāhurdaṃśitaḥ saśarāsanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: The mighty-armed, impetuous, determined Rukmī, dad in armour and armed with a bow, in his high indignation, took the vow in the hearing of all princes (before they departed).

devanagari: अहत्वा समरे कृष्णमप्रत्यूह्य च रुक्मिणीम् । कुण्डिनं न प्रवेक्ष्यामि सत्यमेतद्ब्रवीमि वः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: ahatvā samare kṛṣṇamapratyūhya ca rukmiṇīm kuṇḍinaṃ na pravekṣyāmi satyametadbravīmi vaḥ
translation: “Upon my word I hereby solemnly declare that I shall not enter Kuṇḍinapura unless (and until) I bring back Rukmiṇī after killing Kṛṣṇa in the battle.”

devanagari: इत्युक्त्वा रथमारुह्य सारथिं प्राह सत्वरः । चोदयाश्वान्यतः कृष्णः तस्य मे संयुगं भवेत् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktvā rathamāruhya sārathiṃ prāha satvaraḥ codayāśvānyataḥ kṛṣṇaḥ tasya me saṃyugaṃ bhavet
translation: Announcing this, he mounted his chariot and commanded his charioteer: “Quickly drive the horses to the spot where Kṛṣṇa is, so that I shall have an encounter with him.

devanagari: अद्याहं निशितैर्बाणैर्गोपालस्य सुदुर्मतेः । नेष्ये वीर्यमदं येन स्वसा मे प्रसभं हृता ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: adyāhaṃ niśitairbāṇairgopālasya sudurmateḥ neṣye vīryamadaṃ yena svasā me prasabhaṃ hṛtā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ neṣ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ neṣ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ neṣ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ neṣ class 1 verb]

[nominative single from √ vīr class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ vīr class 10 verb]

translation: Today with my sharp arrows I shall vanquish the pride of valour of that cowherd (or: the protector of the Vedas), the most wicked-minded fellow (or: one who is gracious unto the wicked as well who has abducted per force my sister”.

devanagari: विकत्थमानः कुमतिरीश्वरस्याप्रमाणवित् । रथेनैकेन गोविन्दं तिष्ठ तिष्ठेत्यथाह्वयत् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: vikatthamānaḥ kumatirīśvarasyāpramāṇavit rathenaikena govindaṃ tiṣṭha tiṣṭhetyathāhvayat
translation:
Not knowing the extent of (the infinite) power of the Lord, Rukmī, the wicked braggart pursued alone in a chariot and shouted out the words, ‘Stop’, ‘stop’ to Kṛsṇa who (also) was driving alone in his chariot.
23A. He overtook Kṛṣṇa while he was crossing the divine river Narmaḍā. Being fully prepared for a fight, he repeatedly shouted out at him to stop.

devanagari: धनुर्विकृष्य सुदृढं जघ्ने कृष्णं त्रिभिः शरैः । आह चात्र क्षणं तिष्ठ यदूनां कुलपांसन ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: dhanurvikṛṣya sudṛḍhaṃ jaghne kṛṣṇaṃ tribhiḥ śaraiḥ āha cātra kṣaṇaṃ tiṣṭha yadūnāṃ kulapāṃsana

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: With all the force at his command, he bent the bow (by drawing the bowstring) and hit Kṛṣṇa with three arrows and roared: “Wait minute here (Oh thief! My enemy), the disgrace to Yadu’s family[1].
purport:
Footnote [1]:
As usual Bhāvāratha Dīpikā treats this as a compliment explaining the pun in yadūnāṃ kulapāṃsana as yadūnāṃ kulapa, aṃsana—“you Protector of Yadu’s race, expert in destroying enemies”.

devanagari: यत्र यासि स्वसारं मे मुषित्वा ध्वाङ्क्षवद्धविः । हरिष्येऽद्य मदं मन्द मायिनः कूटयोधिनः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra yāsi svasāraṃ me muṣitvā dhvāṅkṣavaddhaviḥ hariṣye
translation: Where are you going clandestinely kidnapping my sister like a crow[2] stealthily picking up sacrificial oblation? I shall today subdue your pride, you dullard[3] who are a cheat and adopt crooked stratagems in fighting.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā takes muṣiṭvā dhvāṅkṣa as adhvāṅkṣa and interpretes “Like god Indra taking away his legitimate sacrificial oblations”

Footnote [3]:
manda—stable, immovable—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: यावन्न मे हतो बाणैः शयीथा मुञ्च दारिकाम् । स्मयन्कृष्णो धनुश्छित्त्वा षड्भिर्विव्याध रुक्मिणम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: yāvanna me hato bāṇaiḥ śayīthā muñca dārikām smayankṛṣṇo dhanuśchittvā ṣaḍbhirvivyādha rukmiṇam

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Surrender my younger sister (lit. a young girl) before you are not lying (in the dust) struck down by my arrows.”[4] Smilingly Kṛṣṇa cut down Rukmī’s bow and wounded him with six arrows.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
26(A).—Padaratnāvalī’s Text adds:
Bending the bow by drawing the bow-string powerfully, he hit Kṛṣṇa with three arrows.

devanagari: अष्टभिश्चतुरो वाहान्द्वाभ्यां सूतं ध्वजं त्रिभिः । स चान्यद्धनुराधाय कृष्णं विव्याध पञ्चभिः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: aṣṭabhiścaturo vāhāndvābhyāṃ sūtaṃ dhvajaṃ tribhiḥ sa cānyaddhanurādhāya kṛṣṇaṃ vivyādha pañcabhiḥ

translation: He struck his four horses with eight shafts, the charioteer with two and his flag with three. Taking up another bow, Rukmī pierced Kṛṣṇa with five arrows.

devanagari: तैस्तादितः शरौघैस्तु चिच्छेद धनुरच्युतः । पुनरन्यदुपादत्त तदप्यच्छिनदव्ययः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: taistāditaḥ śaraughaistu ciccheda dhanuracyutaḥ punaranyadupādatta tadapyacchinadavyayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: Struck by the stream of arrows, the imperishable Lord Kṛṣṇa sundered Rukmī’s bow. He again equipped himself with (still) another bow which too the Lord snapped.

devanagari: परिघं पट्टिशं शूलं चर्मासी शक्तितोमरौ । यद्यदायुधमादत्त तत्सर्वं सोऽच्छिनद्धरिः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: parighaṃ paṭṭiśaṃ śūlaṃ carmāsī śaktitomarau yadyadāyudhamādatta tatsarvaṃ so

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second single], [imperfect active third single]

translation: Kṛṣṇa cut into splinters whatever weapon—whether a club studded with iron, spear, pike, sword and shield, lance and iron club—Rukmī took up (one after another).

devanagari: ततो रथादवप्लुत्य खड्गपाणिर्जिघांसया । कृष्णमभ्यद्रवत्क्रुद्धः पतङ्ग इव पावकम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: tato rathādavaplutya khaḍgapāṇirjighāṃsayā kṛṣṇamabhyadravatkruddhaḥ pataṅga iva pāvakam
translation: Jumping down from his chariot with a sword in hand, the indignant Rukmī, determined to finish with Kṛṣṇa, rushed at him like unto a moth dashing at a fire.

devanagari: तस्य चापततः खड्गं तिलशश्चर्म चेषुभिः । छित्त्वासिमाददे तिग्मं रुक्मिणं हन्तुमुद्यतः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya cāpatataḥ khaḍgaṃ tilaśaścarma ceṣubhiḥ chittvāsimādade tigmaṃ rukmiṇaṃ hantumudyataḥ
translation: As he was advancing, Kṛṣṇa cut down his sword and shield into sesamum-like particles with his arrows and taking up his sharp sword, he rushed forth bent on killing Rukmī.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा भ्रातृवधोद्योगं रुक्मिणी भयविह्वला । पतित्वा पादयोर्भर्तुरुवाच करुणं सती ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā bhrātṛvadhodyogaṃ rukmiṇī bhayavihvalā patitvā pādayorbharturuvāca karuṇaṃ satī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Beholding his attempt to kill her brother, the virtuous Rukmiṇī was overwhelmed with fear. Falling at the feet of her consort, she piteously[5] entreated:
(Padaratnāvalī’s Text: Rukmiṇī prayed:)

devanagari: श्रीरुक्मिण्युवाच । योगेश्वराप्रमेयात्मन्देवदेव जगत्पते । हन्तुं नार्हसि कल्याण भ्रातरं मे महाभुज ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīrukmiṇyuvāca yogeśvarāprameyātmandevadeva jagatpate hantuṃ nārhasi kalyāṇa bhrātaraṃ me mahābhuja

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
“Oh lord of Yoga of incomprehensible nature! Oh God of gods! The Protector of the world! Oh embodiment of auspiciousness! It does not behove you to kill my brother, Oh mighty-armed Lord.
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text:]
33(A). Praying him thus in frightened tones, the poor Rukmiṇī earnestly desiring to save her brother, clasped the feet of Kṛṣṇa, the Protector of the world.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । तया परित्रासविकम्पिताङ्गया शुचावशुष्यन्मुखरुद्धकण्ठया । कातर्यविस्रंसितहेममालया गृहीतपादः करुणो न्यवर्तत ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca tayā paritrāsavikampitāṅgayā śucāvaśuṣyanmukharuddhakaṇṭhayā kātaryavisraṃsitahemamālayā gṛhītapādaḥ karuṇo nyavartata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[imperfect active third single], [imperfect active third plural]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
The merciful Lord gave up his attempt (on Rukmī’s life) as his feet were clasped by Rukmiṇī whose whole person was trembling with fear, whose mouth was parched up with grief, whose throat was choked (with emotions) and whose gold necklace was fallen, out of nervous anxiety.

devanagari: चैलेन बद्ध्वा तमसाधुकारिणं सश्मश्रुकेशं प्रवपन्व्यरूपयत् । तावन्ममर्दुः परसैन्यमद्भुतं यदुप्रवीरा नलिनीं यथा गजाः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: cailena baddhvā tamasādhukāriṇaṃ saśmaśrukeśaṃ pravapanvyarūpayat tāvanmamarduḥ parasainyamadbhutaṃ yadupravīrā nalinīṃ yathā gajāḥ
translation: Binding that evil-doer with a piece of cloth, he disfigured him by shaving his head (with the blade of his sword) keeping (intermittently) some part of the mustaches and hairlocks on the head. In the meanwhile, prominent heroes in the Yādava forces crushed and routed the extraordinary hostile forces, like elephants trampling down stalks of lotuses.

devanagari: कृष्णान्तिकमुपव्रज्य ददृशुस्तत्र रुक्मिणम् । तथाभूतं हतप्रायं दृष्ट्वा सङ्कर्षणो विभुः । विमुच्य बद्धं करुणो भगवान्कृष्णमब्रवीत् ॥ ३६ ...
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇāntikamupavrajya dadṛśustatra rukmiṇam tathābhūtaṃ hataprāyaṃ dṛṣṭvā saṅkarṣaṇo vibhuḥ vimucya baddhaṃ karuṇo bhagavānkṛṣṇamabravīt
translation:
When they approached to the presence of Kṛṣṇa, they beheld Rukmī reduced to that (humiliating) plight, as good as dead (through shame). The mighty Lord Balarāma was moved with pity and releasing him, he protested to Kṛṣṇa.
[Padaratnāvalī’s Text: Balarāma reproved:]

devanagari: असाध्विदं त्वया कृष्ण कृतमस्मज्जुगुप्सितम् । वपनं श्मश्रुकेशानां वैरूप्यं सुहृदो वधः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: asādhvidaṃ tvayā kṛṣṇa kṛtamasmajjugupsitam vapanaṃ śmaśrukeśānāṃ vairūpyaṃ suhṛdo vadhaḥ
translation: “This blame-worthy act, abhorrent to us (our race), has been perpetrated by you, Oh Kṛṣṇa, in shaving the moustaches and the locks of hair of a relative. Disfiguring a relative this way amounts to killing him.

devanagari: मैवास्मान्साध्व्यसूयेथा भ्रातुर्वैरूप्यचिन्तया । सुखदुःखदो न चान्योऽस्ति यतः स्वकृतभुक्पुमान् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: maivāsmānsādhvyasūyethā bhrāturvairūpyacintayā sukhaduḥkhado na cānyo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: (Addressing Rukmiṇī): In your grief and brooding over this deformity of your brother, please do not scorn us (as evil-minded people) Oh good lady. No other person is the giver of (i.e. responsible for) the pleasure or pain that comes to one’s lot; for a person reaps the fruits of his own actions.

devanagari: बन्धुर्वधार्हदोषोऽपि न बन्धोर्वधमर्हति । त्याज्यः स्वेनैव दोषेण हतः किं हन्यते पुनः ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: bandhurvadhārhadoṣo tyājyaḥ svenaiva doṣeṇa hataḥ kiṃ hanyate punaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ tyaj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ tyaj ]
[nominative single from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: Even if a relative has committed an offence deserving death in retribution, he should not be killed by a relative. He should be discharged forthwith. What is the propriety of slaying a person again, when he stands killed by his own sinful acts.

devanagari: क्षत्रियाणामयं धर्मः प्रजापतिविनिर्मितः । भ्रातापि भ्रातरं हन्याद्येन घोरतमस्ततः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: kṣatriyāṇāmayaṃ dharmaḥ prajāpativinirmitaḥ bhrātāpi bhrātaraṃ hanyādyena ghoratamastataḥ
translation: God Brahmā has ordained this as the dharma (the code of conduct) for Kṣattriyas that even a brother should kill a brother (in the battle). What could be more terrible than this? (Hence it is the kṣattriya dharma that is at fault and not we who abide by it, Oh lady).

devanagari: राज्यस्य भूमेर्वित्तस्य स्त्रियो मानस्य तेजसः । मानिनोऽन्यस्य वा हेतोः श्रीमदान्धाः क्षिपन्ति हि ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: rājyasya bhūmervittasya striyo mānasya tejasaḥ mānino

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ rāj ]
[genitive single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ rāj ]
[genitive single from √ man class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ man class 8 verb]
[genitive single from √ man class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative plural from √ kṣip class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ kṣip class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ kṣip class 6 verb]

translation: (Turning to Kṛṣṇa) It is only the proud, blinded with the intoxicating influence of prosperity that offend relatives for the sake of kingdom, land, wealth woman, honour, authority or for any such consideration.

devanagari: तवेयं विषमा बुद्धिः सर्वभूतेषु दुर्हृदाम् । यन्मन्यसे सदाभद्रं सुहृदां भद्रमज्ञवत् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: taveyaṃ viṣamā buddhiḥ sarvabhūteṣu durhṛdām yanmanyase sadābhadraṃ suhṛdāṃ bhadramajñavat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[present middle second single], [present passive second single]

translation: (Addressing Rukmiṇī): It is an improper lookout on your part that you, like an ignorant person, think auspiciously and well of your brother who always bears ill-will towards all beings and ill of those who are your well-wishers[6].
purport:
Footnote [6]:
SR adds: It is certainly your improper outlook that you regard the auspicious or proper punishment meted out to your brother (by shaving him) as evil, even though your relative is ill-disposed to all beings.

devanagari: आत्ममोहो नृणामेव कल्पते देवमायया । सुहृद्दुर्हृदुदासीन इति देहात्ममानिनाम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmamoho nṛṇāmeva kalpate devamāyayā suhṛddurhṛdudāsīna iti dehātmamāninām
translation: “That some persons are friends, some enemies and some indifferent is the self-delusion created by Lord’s Māyā (deluding potency) among persons who regard the body and the soul as identical.

devanagari: एक एव परो ह्यात्मा सर्वेषामपि देहिनाम् । नानेव गृह्यते मूढैर्यथा ज्योतिर्यथा नभः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: eka eva paro hyātmā sarveṣāmapi dehinām nāneva gṛhyate mūḍhairyathā jyotiryathā nabhaḥ
translation: For the Supreme Soul is one only in all corporeal beings. But he is mistaken as many by the ignorant, just as the luminary (the sun, though one, is regarded as many when it is reflected indifferent vessels containing water) or the sky (though one, is regarded as different when circumscribed in a pitcher (ghaṭākāśa) or within a room (maṭhākāśa) is looked upon as many (when in reality it is one).

devanagari: देह आद्यन्तवानेष द्रव्यप्राणगुणात्मकः । आत्मन्यविद्यया कॢप्तः संसारयति देहिनम् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: deha ādyantavāneṣa dravyaprāṇaguṇātmakaḥ ātmanyavidyayā kḷptaḥ saṃsārayati dehinam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: This body constituted as it is of five material elements (like the earth, water, fire and others), vital elements (like sense-organs and their presiding deities) and the guṇas[7] and has a beginning and an end, is projected on the Soul (ātman) through ignorance (avidyā) which makes the embodied soul wander in the cycle of saṃsāra[8].
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: and is thus conditioned by the triad—ādhibhautika, ādhyātmika and ādhidaivika.

Footnote [8]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: The body is not pure due to conditioning circumstances.

devanagari: नात्मनोऽन्येन संयोगो वियोगश्च सतः सति । तद्धेतुत्वात्तत्प्रसिद्धेर्दृग्रूपाभ्यां यथा रवेः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: nātmano taddhetutvāttatprasiddherdṛgrūpābhyāṃ yathā raveḥ
translation: The soul has no union with or disunion from anything else (viz. the body), as nothing other than the soul exists at all, Oh good lady. The soul is the cause of such appearance, just as the sun is the cause of the perception of the sun by means of the eye and colour (but the sun bears no union or disunion with them)[9].
purport:
Footnote [9]:
(i) It is the power of illumination of the sun which shines all the while and has no contact with the eye and as such has neither union or disunion with the eye. Similarly, the Soul being of the nature of consciousness and existing in the past, present and future has no possibility of union or disunion with the body—VT.
(ii) The Supreme Soul is not bound or separated from the body coming into existence of which is dependent on Him, just as the Sun is unconnected with the eye or the colour, both of which depend on the sun for their function—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: जन्मादयस्तु देहस्य विक्रिया नात्मनः क्वचित् । कलानामिव नैवेन्दोर्मृतिर्ह्यस्य कुहूरिव ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: janmādayastu dehasya vikriyā nātmanaḥ kvacit kalānāmiva naivendormṛtirhyasya kuhūriva
translation: Changes such as birth (youth, death) and others pertain to the body and never to the soul, just as the waxing and the waning of the moon are attributable to the digits of the moon (which remains constant all the while). Theḍeath of the body is like the amāvasyā (kuhū) when the light of the moon disappears but the moon remains unaffected (in the same way the soul continues to exist despite the disappearance of the body).

devanagari: यथा शयान आत्मानं विषयान्फलमेव च । अनुभुङ्क्तेऽप्यसत्यर्थे तथाप्नोत्यबुधो भवम् ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā śayāna ātmānaṃ viṣayānphalameva ca anubhuṅkte

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ śī class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ śī class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ śī class 2 verb], [locative single from √ śī class 2 verb]
[nominative dual from √ śī class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ śī class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ śī class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ śī class 2 verb]

translation: Just as a sleeping man, in a dream, perceives himself, the sense objects of enjoyment and experiences the fruit (enjoyment) thereof, even though nothing of these exists in reality, similarly an ignorant person undergoes (the experience of this unreal) saṃsāra.

devanagari: तस्मादज्ञानजं शोकमात्मशोषविमोहनम् । तत्त्वज्ञानेन निर्हृत्य स्वस्था भव शुचिस्मिते ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmādajñānajaṃ śokamātmaśoṣavimohanam tattvajñānena nirhṛtya svasthā bhava śucismite
translation: Therefore, by means of the knowledge of reality eliminate completely your sorrow which is born of ignorance and which causes you pining and delusion. And be comforted and serene at heart, Oh lady, with bright smiles.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं भगवता तन्वी रामेण प्रतिबोधिता । वैमनस्यं परित्यज्य मनो बुद्ध्या समादधे ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ bhagavatā tanvī rāmeṇa pratibodhitā vaimanasyaṃ parityajya mano buddhyā samādadhe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
When the beautifully slim lady was thus enlightened by the glorious Lord Balarāma, she overcame her mental depression and attained equipoise of her mind through reason.

devanagari: प्राणावशेष उत्सृष्टो द्विड्भिर्हतबलप्रभः । स्मरन्विरूपकरणं वितथात्ममनोरथः ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: prāṇāvaśeṣa utsṛṣṭo dviḍbhirhatabalaprabhaḥ smaranvirūpakaraṇaṃ vitathātmamanorathaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Allowed to get away with life only, shorn of his power (or army) as well as of glory, brooding over his disfigurement and with his plans and ambitions frustrated, Rukmī built a big town called Bhojakaṭa[10] for his residence.
purport:
Footnote [10]:
Bhojakaṭa or Bhojapur is identified with Bhojapura, six miles to the south-east of Vidiśā. GDAMI 33. It was built to the north of the Narmadā as per purāṇas which thus support this identification. GDAMI 33 and 224.

devanagari: चक्रे भोजकटं नाम निवासाय महत्पुरम् । अहत्वा दुर्मतिं कृष्णमप्रत्यूह्य यवीयसीम् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: cakre bhojakaṭaṃ nāma nivāsāya mahatpuram ahatvā durmatiṃ kṛṣṇamapratyūhya yavīyasīm

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: He resided there through indignation, as he formerly announced. “I shall not re-enter Kuṇḍina unless and until I have killed the wicked-minded Kṛṣṇa and brought back my younger sister”.

devanagari: कुण्डिनं न प्रवेक्ष्यामीत्युक्त्वा तत्रावसद्रुषा । भगवान्भीष्मकसुतामेवं निर्जित्य भूमिपान् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: kuṇḍinaṃ na pravekṣyāmītyuktvā tatrāvasadruṣā bhagavānbhīṣmakasutāmevaṃ nirjitya bhūmipān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: Having vanquished all the monarchs in his way, the Lord brought Rukmiṇī, the daughter of Bhīṣmaka to his city, Dvārakā and married her as per Śāstric injunction, Oh Scion of the Kuru race.

devanagari: अथ चतुःपञ्चाशत्तमोऽध्यायः 10. ॥ 54 ॥
english_devnagari: atha catuḥpañcāśattamo
translation: There was a great festivity in every house in Dvārakā, the capital of Yadus, of the residents thereof, who were exclusively devoted to Lord Kṛṣṇa, the protector of Yadus, Oh king.

devanagari: नरा नार्यश्च मुदिताः प्रमृष्टमणिकुण्डलाः । पारिबर्हमुपाजह्रुर्वरयोश्चित्रवाससोः ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: narā nāryaśca muditāḥ pramṛṣṭamaṇikuṇḍalāḥ pāribarhamupājahrurvarayościtravāsasoḥ

[nominative plural from √ mud class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ mud class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ mud class 10 verb]

translation: Men and women thereof were highly delighted. Adorned with ear-rings of highly polished jewels, they presented valuable gifts to the newly-wedded couple wearing excellent bridal dress.

devanagari: सा वृष्णिपुर्युत्तम्भितेन्द्र केतुभिर् । विचित्रमाल्याम्बररत्नतोरणैः । बभौ प्रतिद्वार्युपकॢप्तमङ्गलैर् । आपूर्णकुम्भागुरुधूपदीपकैः ॥ ५...
english_devnagari: sā vṛṣṇipuryuttambhitendra ketubhir vicitramālyāmbararatnatoraṇaiḥ babhau pratidvāryupakḷptamaṅgalair āpūrṇakumbhāgurudhūpadīpakaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The city of Vṛṣṇis (Dvārakā) shone splendidly with flags raised in honour of Indra, with a wonderful variety of garlands, cloths and ornamental arches decorated with jewels, provided with auspicious arrangement at every door of pitchers full of water, insense of the aguru (agallochum) burning and lamps.

devanagari: सिक्तमार्गा मदच्युद्भिराहूतप्रेष्ठभूभुजाम् । गजैर्द्वाःसु परामृष्ट रम्भापूगोपशोभिता ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: siktamārgā madacyudbhirāhūtapreṣṭhabhūbhujām gajairdvāḥsu parāmṛṣṭa rambhāpūgopaśobhitā
translation: Its streets were besprinkled with the exuded ichor (temporal fluid) of elephants of the great kings invited for the marriage, and entrances beautified with tall plantain trees and areca-nut trees.

devanagari: कुरुसृञ्जयकैकेय विदर्भयदुकुन्तयः । मिथो मुमुदिरे तस्मिन्सम्भ्रमात्परिधावताम् ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: kurusṛñjayakaikeya vidarbhayadukuntayaḥ mitho mumudire tasminsambhramātparidhāvatām
translation: Moving hastily here and there in confusion in that marriage, clansmen of Kurus, Sṛñjayas, Kaikeyas, Vidarbhas, Yadus and Kuntīs were happy to meet each other.

devanagari: रुक्मिण्या हरणं श्रुत्वा गीयमानं ततस्ततः । राजानो राजकन्याश्च बभूवुर्भृशविस्मिताः ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: rukmiṇyā haraṇaṃ śrutvā gīyamānaṃ tatastataḥ rājāno rājakanyāśca babhūvurbhṛśavismitāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: Hearing the episode of Kṛṣṇa’s carrying away Rukmiṇī sung by the people everywhere, kings and princesses who attended the marriage became extremely wonder-struck.

devanagari: द्वारकायामभूद्राजन्महामोदः पुरौकसाम् । रुक्मिण्या रमयोपेतं दृष्ट्वा कृष्णं श्रियः पतिम् ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: dvārakāyāmabhūdrājanmahāmodaḥ puraukasām rukmiṇyā ramayopetaṃ dṛṣṭvā kṛṣṇaṃ śriyaḥ patim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation:
Great was the exultation among the citizens of Dvārakā, Oh king, to see Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of goddess Śrī now married to Rukmiṇī who was no other than goddess Lakṣmī herself.[11]
[back to top]
purport:
Footnote [11]:
Ekanātha has written an excellent work in ovī metre on Rukmiṇī’s marriage. Out of respect for Ekanātha, KD has incorporated it in his com. on this skandha (Harivaradā)
